records.ety: [OE. diocise, OF. diocise, F. diocse, L. dioecesis, fr. Gr. <?/ housekeeping, administration, a province, a diocese, fr. <?/ to keep house, manage; dia` through + <?/ to manage a household, <?/ a house. See Economy.]

defs: The circuit or extent of a bishop's jurisdiction; the district in which a bishop exercises his ecclesiastical authority.
